entries.content: Session Apr 23-24: 52 commits. Fixed AI scripting pipeline (7 layers), self-improvement engine, universal learning, AI XP system, chat UX overhaul, space theme, plugin redesign, OpenRouter multi-model, project labels, Discord cleanup.
Gemini and Groq free tiers exhaust daily with 10+ testers. Always race 4 providers: Groq > Claude > Gemini > OpenRouter. Free quotas reset at midnight UTC.
Fallback templates were generating houses for everything. REMOVED all fallbacks — AI now either generates what was asked or tells user honestly it failed.
Competitors: Lemonade.gg uses OpenRouter 372B tokens/mo. Ropilot has playtesting. Rebirth is cheap at $8.99. Our edge: 200 agents, all-inclusive, self-improving AI.

ELI bot was spamming bug/suggestion channels with Quality Report embeds. FIXED: acknowledgeInChannel disabled, AUTO_RESPOND_CHANNELS reduced to bot-only channels, SILENT_CHANNELS blocks bug/suggestion channels entirely.
Pricing changed to 5 paid tiers: Starter $10 (5K), Builder $25 (15K), Creator $50 (40K), Pro $150 (100K), Studio $200 (200K). Builder and Pro need Stripe price IDs.
Plugin UI simplified from 1201 to 732 lines. Now just: code box + connect + undo. Space theme background.
# Claude Preferences — How to Work with Dawse

Who is Dawse
- **Name**: Yomi / Dawse, 20 years old (turns 21 Aug 16, 2026)
- **Role**: Solo founder & developer of ForjeGames
- **Budget**: Zero — bootstrapping everything
- **Day job**: Semi part-time jet detailing
- **Location**: Windows 11, project at `C:\dev\roblox-map-building`
- **Has testers**: A few (never met most in person), but platform not ready yet
- **Never published a Roblox game** — ForjeGames is his first major project
- **Considering building Roblox games** to generate revenue while platform grows

Working Style — CRITICAL
- **MAX AUTONOMY** — commit, push, deploy, edit without asking. EVER.
- **"Do it for me"** — Dawse wants Claude to execute, not explain steps
- **Visual thinker** — "put it on my screen", "load it so I can see"
- **Repeats when frustrated** — same message 2-4x means something is broken
- **"Fix it all"** = find every bug and fix in one pass
- **"Make it frictionless"** = zero unnecessary clicks, loading, dead buttons
- **"Take full control"** = make all decisions yourself
- Don't summarize — he can read the diff
- Bundled PRs fine — don't split refactors into many small ones

What He HATES
1. Corporate AI language — "stunning", "captivating", "vibrant"
2. SmoothPlastic — flat and cheap. Use Wood, Brick, Concrete, Metal
3. Single-part builds — everything needs multi-part detail
4. Manual steps — don't explain, just DO it
5. Crashes — save handoffs frequently
6. Silent failures — buttons that do nothing
7. Repetition — having to explain the same thing twice

What He LOVES
1. Speed — commit, push, move on
2. Detail — multi-part builds, realistic lighting, textured materials
3. Space aesthetic — dark theme, floating neon orbs, stars, depth
4. Fun for kids — bright colors, oversized windows, welcoming feel
5. Imagination — "build anything" not just templates
6. Competitor awareness — know what others do, do it better
7. Initiative — "I also added X because Y"

Communication Decoder
- Types fast with typos: "evrything" = everything, "extreas" = extras
- ALL CAPS word = the priority
- "please" after 2nd ask = frustrated
- Short msgs = fine. Long msgs = important context.
- Pastes huge crashed-session blocks = extract what you need, don't complain

Technical Knowledge
- Comfortable with Next.js, React, TypeScript, Prisma, Stripe
- Knows Roblox ecosystem and Studio plugin development
- Understands AI model routing and multi-provider architectures

Session Management
- Save handoffs every 30 minutes
- Max 2 parallel agents (crashes at 5+)
- Keep bash output short (| head -20)
- No screenshots unless absolutely needed

Key Paths
- Project: `C:\dev\roblox-map-building`
- Live: `https://forjegames.com`
- Vault: `C:\Users\Dawse\Documents\ForjeVault\`
- Plugin: `packages/studio-plugin/` (NOT `src/plugin/`)
- Type-check: `tsconfig.spotcheck.json` (full tsc OOMs)

# AI Provider Orchestrator

**Core file**: `src/lib/ai/provider.ts`
**Function**: `callAI()` — routes requests to the best available model

How It Works

```
User prompt
  → Free enhancement via Groq (llama-3.3-70b)
  → Route to primary model (Gemini by default)
  → Fallback chain if primary fails
  → Structured output (jsonMode) or streaming
  → Token tracking + billing
```

Provider Priority
1. **Gemini 2.0 Flash** — primary, jsonMode, fast, cheap
2. **Groq llama-3.3-70b** — free prompt enhancement layer, fallback
3. **Claude** — optional, user-provided API key
4. **OpenAI** — specific model tasks

9 AI Agent Modes
The orchestrator powers 9 distinct agent modes (more than any competitor):
1. Script generation (Luau code)
2. Map/world building
3. UI/GUI design
4. Game mechanics
5. Asset generation (images)
6. 3D mesh generation (Meshy)
7. Terrain generation
8. City/layout architecture
9. Playtest analysis

Image Generation
- **FAL** with Stable Diffusion backend
- 13 preset styles defined in `src/lib/image-styles.ts`
- Styles include: realistic, anime, pixel art, low-poly, etc.

3D Mesh Generation
- **Meshy** text-to-3D API
- UNIQUE to ForjeGames — no competitor offers this
- Generates .fbx/.obj meshes from text descriptions

Token Economy
- Subscription tiers include token allocations
- Token packs available via Stripe
- Token refund on AI error (5 call sites in chat/route.ts — PENDING implementation)

Key Patterns
- P() helper function for prompt construction — AI must recognize this
- Structured commands translator converts AI output to Studio plugin commands
- False "Built!" claims fixed (Apr 12) — AI no longer claims success without verification

| 5 | 20260406000000_add_pgvector_rag | RAG embeddings (BLOCKED — pgvector not installed on Neon) |

Key Models (from schema)

- **Subscription** — Stripe-linked, currentPeriodEnd for tier-guard expiry

- **BetaInvite** — Invite code system

Known Issues
- **pgvector blocker**: Migration 5 requires pgvector extension installed on Neon. Fix: run `CREATE EXTENSION vector;` via Neon console, or use shadow DB URL pointing to Docker postgres with pgvector.
- Shadow DB for local migrations: `docker-compose.shadow-db.yml`

Seed Data
- `prisma/seed.ts` — initial data seeding

# MCP Agents

ForjeGames uses Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers for specialized AI agent tasks.

Studio Bridge (`packages/mcp/studio-bridge`)
- **Purpose**: Cloud relay between Roblox Studio plugin and web app
- **Protocol**: WebSocket with HTTP fallback
- **Auth**: JWT (shared secret between plugin and server)
- Routes commands from the web editor to Studio and back

Studio Controller (`packages/mcp/`, port 3006)
- **Purpose**: Local Studio control for development
- **Port**: 3006
- Direct connection to Studio for testing

Asset Alchemist (`packages/mcp/asset-alchemist`)
- **Purpose**: AI agent for generating game assets
- **Capabilities**: Image generation (FAL), 3D mesh (Meshy), script generation
- **Package**: Has own package.json

City Architect (`packages/mcp/city-architect`)
- **Purpose**: AI agent for city and map layout generation
- **Capabilities**: Procedural city layouts, building placement, road networks
- **Package**: Has own package.json

Terrain Forge (`packages/mcp/terrain-forge`)
- **Purpose**: AI agent for terrain generation
- **Capabilities**: Heightmaps, biomes, terrain painting
- **Package**: Has own package.json

9-Agent Orchestrator
The web app orchestrates all 9 AI modes through `callAI()`:
1. Script gen → direct Gemini/Groq
2. Map building → city-architect MCP
3. UI/GUI → direct AI
4. Game mechanics → direct AI
5. Asset gen → asset-alchemist MCP (FAL)
6. 3D mesh → asset-alchemist MCP (Meshy)
7. Terrain → terrain-forge MCP
8. City layout → city-architect MCP
9. Playtest analysis → direct AI + Studio bridge

Future MCP Plans
- CLI npm package (match Ropilot's `@ropilot/mcp-cli`)
- Python agent SDK integration
- More specialized agents (animation, VFX, sound design)

Plugin Files

12 Structured Commands
The plugin handles these command types (no loadstring):
1. create_part
2. create_model
3. modify_properties
4. delete_instance
5. create_script
6. structured_commands (batch)
7. start_playtest
8. stop_playtest

10. capture_screenshot
11. create_sound
12. scan_workspace (returns scene manifest to server)

Build Process
```bash
node packages/studio-plugin/build-plugin.js
# Outputs: public/plugin/ForjeGames.rbxmx (157KB)
# Auto-installs to: %LOCALAPPDATA%/Roblox/Plugins/
```

Connection Flow
```
Studio Plugin
  → WebSocket to studio-bridge (cloud relay)
  → HTTP fallback if WS fails
  → JWT auth (shared secret between plugin and server)
  → Commands dispatched via dispatchCommand()
  → Results returned as tuples (fixed: was being discarded in v4.5)
```

Known Issues (Fixed)
- dispatchCommand return tuple was discarded (fixed v4.6.0)
- scan_workspace results never reached server (fixed v4.6.0)
- Plugin needs rebuild + republish after Lua changes
- CDN edge cache on public/plugin/*.rbxmx can serve stale builds

# Pattern — Audit Agent False Positives

Rule
Haiku-powered audit subagents have a **~50% false positive rate**. Always verify each claim against real code lines before editing.

The Three Worst Offenders
1. **"Missing input validation"** — often the validation exists in middleware or Clerk
2. **"SQL injection risk"** — Prisma parameterizes queries automatically
3. **"Missing error handling"** — the error handling exists but in a parent try/catch

How to Apply
When running security/code audits with Claude Haiku:
1. Get the report
2. For each finding, grep the actual file and line
3. Verify the issue exists in the current code
4. Only fix confirmed issues
5. Expect to discard ~half the findings
# Pattern — Next.js Public File Tracing

The Problem
API routes that read from `public/` via `fs.readFileSync` work locally but serve **stale files on Vercel** because Next.js doesn't automatically include `public/` files in serverless function bundles.

Why This Matters
- Cost us 6 hours of debugging
- Plugin .rbxmx was being served from CDN edge cache instead of the actual file
- Applies to ANY API route that reads from public/ at runtime

When to Apply
Any time you create an API route that uses `fs.readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), 'public', ...))`.
# Pattern — Parallel Claude Sessions

How to Detect
- Check `git status` for unexpected changes
- Look for files modified in the last few minutes that you didn't touch
- Check if another Claude session is running in task manager

Safe vs Unsafe Edit Zones
During auth work, these zones are safe for parallel editing:
- Marketing pages
- Documentation
- Test files
- Unrelated API routes

**UNSAFE** (will conflict):
- middleware.ts
- Auth components
- Clerk config
- Shared layout files

Rules
- Always explicit-stage-by-name (`git add src/specific-file.ts`)
- Never `git add -A` or `git add .`
- Check git status before committing
- If you see unexpected changes, don't overwrite — ask the user

# Pattern — Vercel Hobby Limits

ForjeGames is on Vercel Hobby plan. Know these limits:

| Limit | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Cron frequency | **Daily only** (no hourly/minute crons) |
| Function timeout | **300 seconds** max |
| Build size | **100MB** max |
| Bandwidth | 100GB/mo |
| Serverless executions | 100K/mo |

Implications
- Stripe reconcile cron runs daily, not hourly
- Long AI generation tasks must complete within 300s or use streaming
- Keep build size lean — watch node_modules and public/ assets
- If we need sub-daily crons, upgrade to Pro ($20/mo) or use Upstash QStash

# BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend (Apr 13)
#bugfix #critical #solved
Date: 2026-04-13
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk0

entries.content:
The Problem
User said "AI gives shitty models" and "nothing loads into Studio."
The AI was actually generating 4,000+ chars of valid Luau code — but it was silently dropped.
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk1

entries.content:
Root Cause
Two-pass free model pipeline (Gemini Flash + Groq Llama):
- Pass 1: Conversational response (explains what it'll build)
- Pass 2: Luau code generation (actual buildable code)

The code from Pass 2 was generated correctly, but **6 response paths** (3 JSON + 3 stream) omitted the `luauCode` field from the response. Frontend saw `hasCode: true` but had no code.

Additionally, the frontend's useChat hook only extracted code by regex-matching ` ```lua ``` ` blocks in the stream text. Since the two-pass pipeline sends conversation text (no code blocks) in the stream and code separately, the regex never matched.
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk2

entries.content:
The Fix (commits 053b3c2 + fb11b46)
1. **Backend**: Added `luauCode` field to all 6 response paths (gemini-flash JSON/stream, groq-llama JSON/stream, auto-cascade JSON/stream)
2. **Frontend**: useChat now checks `meta.luauCode` first (from server), falls back to code block extraction
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk3

entries.content:
Files Changed
- `src/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts` — 6 response paths fixed
- `src/app/(app)/editor/hooks/useChat.ts` — meta.luauCode extraction
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk4

entries.content:
Impact
Before: AI said "I built a house" → nothing happened → user thought output was garbage
After: AI says "I built a house" → 4,120 chars of valid Luau code returned → can be imported to Studio
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk5

entries.content:
Lesson
Always include the generated artifact in the API response, even when it's "also" being sent to Studio. If the plugin isn't connected, the code must still reach the frontend for manual import.
entries.source: obsidian:BUG FIX — luauCode Never Returned to Frontend.md#chunk6

entries.content:
# Competitive Intelligence Report
#competitors #strategy #research
Date: 2026-04-13

TL;DR

We analyzed 5 direct competitors. **ForjeGames has the most sophisticated tech** but competitors are winning on marketing, pricing, and user friction.

| Competitor | Users Claimed | Price | AI Model | Studio Plugin | Our Edge |
|-----------|--------------|-------|----------|--------------|----------|
| Ropilot | Unknown | $20-250/mo + YOUR API costs | BYOK (you pay OpenAI/Anthropic) | Desktop app required | We include AI — one bill |
| Rebirth | 50K+ | $8.99-15.99/mo | Hidden | Yes (Creator Store) | We have 9 agents, mesh gen, image gen |
| Lemonade | 100K+ | $20/mo (100 prompts) | Hidden (Gemini suspected) | Yes (Creator Store) | We're faster, more modes |
| ForgeGUI | ~28K | $15.99-200/mo | Hidden | No plugin | We actually build in Studio |
| BloxToolkit | Unknown | $19-79/mo | GPT | No plugin | We're real, they have fake testimonials |

The Uncomfortable Truth

**None of these tools can build production games.** The best AI (Gemini 3.1 Pro) fails 45% of Roblox tasks on first attempt. Every tool is a scripting assistant with varying Studio integration.

What We Have That Nobody Else Does
- 9-agent orchestrator (multi-agent coordination)
- 9 AI modes (build/think/plan/image/script/terrain/mesh/debug/idea)
- 13 image generation styles including Roblox clothing UV maps
- 3D mesh generation (Meshy)
- Canvas editor (Canva-like)
- Robux payments
- Free prompt enhancement (Groq Llama)
- Plugin ChangeHistory undo (single Ctrl+Z)
- 50+ curated marketplace assets
- Automated playtest + input simulation + AI visual verification

Ropilot (ropilot.ai)
- **Company**: ROSUITE LLC, Newark DE. Founded by "ClassifiedCoach" — started as open-source plugin Sept 2024
- **Model**: BYOK — users bring own API keys. Users pay Ropilot + pay OpenAI/Anthropic separately
- **Architecture**: Desktop app (required) + Studio plugin + npm MCP CLI
- **Reality**: Scripting copilot only. Cannot modify models, sounds, meshes. Zero documented user-built games.
- **Pricing**: Free 250 actions, $20-250/mo (+ your API costs = $40-300+ total)

Rebirth (userebirth.com)
- **Company**: England-based, unknown team
- **Stack**: Next.js + Turbopack, Supabase Auth, Fly.io backend
- **Reality**: Script generator. Their own docs warn "chain small prompts" because complex requests fail.
- **Pricing**: $8.99/mo (100 prompts), $15.99/mo (300 prompts)
- **Marketing**: TikTok active, 6 SEO blog posts, Rewardful affiliates

Lemonade (lemonade.gg)
- **Company**: San Francisco, founder @nicolasvizi
- **Auth**: Roblox OAuth (zero friction — genius move)
- **Reality**: In their own demo, cats fell through floors, NPCs walked off maps. "Boilerplate only."
- **Pricing**: 1 free credit/day, $20/mo for 100 prompts ($0.20/prompt = expensive)

ForgeGUI (forgegui.com)
- **Company**: ForgeGUI Inc., built with bolt.new
- **Reality**: Image-only tool. No Studio plugin. No code generation. Generates thumbnails/icons/clothing.
- **Has**: Public remix gallery, prompt guide page

BloxToolkit (bloxtoolkit.com)
- **Trust**: 0/100 on ScamAdviser. FAKE testimonials (DevForum avatars + fabricated names)
- **Reality**: Web-only, no Studio integration. GPT wrapper with Roblox skin.
- **Pricing**: $19-79/mo

Key Gaps to Close
1. Add Roblox OAuth (Lemonade's #1 advantage)
2. Restructure pricing ($9.99/$19.99/$29.99)
3. Affiliate program (Rewardful)
4. TikTok marketing
5. 6 SEO blog posts

Full Analysis
See `C:\dev\competitor-mirrors\COMPETITIVE_ANALYSIS.md` for the complete 400+ line report with tech stack details, Supabase URLs, Firebase configs, and all source code findings.

# Pricing Analysis — ForjeGames vs Market
#pricing #strategy #business
Date: 2026-04-13

Current Market Pricing

| Tool | Free Tier | Entry | Mid | Top | AI Included? |
|------|----------|-------|-----|-----|-------------|
| **ForjeGames** | Yes (Free $0) | $9.99/mo (Hobby) | $24.99/mo (Creator) | $49.99/mo (Studio) | YES |
| **Ropilot** | 250 actions | $20/mo | $50/mo | $250/mo | NO (you pay OpenAI/Anthropic too) |
| **Rebirth** | 5 credits | $8.99/mo | $15.99/mo | $15.99/mo | YES |
| **Lemonade** | 1/day | $20/mo | $20/mo | $20/mo | YES |
| **ForgeGUI** | 5 credits | $15.99/mo | $50/mo | $200/mo | YES |
| **BloxToolkit** | None | $19/mo | $39/mo | $79/mo | YES |

Current Pricing (Implemented)

| Tier | Price | Target |
|------|-------|--------|
| Free | $0 | Try before buy |
| Hobby | $9.99/mo | Compete with Rebirth Hobby |
| Creator | $24.99/mo | Compete with Rebirth Pro + Lemonade |
| Studio | $49.99/mo | Power users, teams |

Key Insight
Ropilot's BYOK model means their true cost is $40-300+/mo. We can message:
**"All-inclusive AI. One bill. No API keys needed."**

Rebirth's $8.99 is the floor. We need a $9.99 tier to compete.

Roblox Audience Reality
- Most Roblox developers are 13-22 years old
- $30/mo is a LOT for a teenager
- $9.99/mo is "one month of allowance"
- Free tier with generous limits drives word-of-mouth

Target
**$30K/mo** = 1,000 Studio tier subscribers at $30/mo

Pricing Tiers
| Tier | Price | Positioning |
|------|-------|-------------|
| Free | $0 | Try it, limited tokens |
| Starter | $9.99/mo | Casual builders |
| Builder | $19.99/mo | Regular creators |
| Studio | $29.99/mo | Power users (target tier) |
| Team | Custom | Studios & groups |
| Enterprise | Custom | Large organizations |

**Key insight**: Rebirth charges $8.99/mo and has 50K users. Our $30/mo is 3.3x more — justified by 9 agents, 3D mesh, 13 styles, but messaging must make this obvious.

Acquisition Channels (Zero Budget)
1. **Twitter/X** — dev community, daily Roblox AI clips
2. **YouTube Shorts** — 60-second build demos (script: YOUTUBE-DEMO-SCRIPT.md)
3. **TikTok** — follow Rebirth's playbook, short AI build timelapses
4. **SEO** — 6 blog posts targeting "roblox ai builder", "roblox script generator"
5. **DevForum** — Roblox developer community posts
6. **Influencer partnerships** — Roblox content creators, rev-share
7. **Affiliate program** — Rewardful integration (Ropilot & Rebirth both have this)

Conversion Levers
- Paywall UX (clear upgrade path)
- Usage meter (show token consumption)
- Watermarks on free-tier outputs
- First-build magic (onboarding wow moment)
- Social proof (user count, testimonials)

90-Day Plan
| Phase | Timeline | Goal |
|-------|----------|------|
| Soft launch | Days 1-30 | 100 users, $50 MRR |
| Public launch | Days 31-60 | 1K users, $500 MRR |
| Paid ads | Days 61-90 | 10K users, $5K MRR |

Competitive Moat
ForjeGames is the ONLY all-inclusive platform:
- Competitors force BYOK (bring your own API key) = friction
- Competitors charge per-prompt ($0.20/prompt at Lemonade) = unpredictable costs
- We include everything: 9 AI modes, image gen, 3D mesh, canvas editor, Studio plugin

# Competitive Intelligence Report — ForjeGames vs 5 Competitors

Generated April 13, 2026 — FINAL VERSION (all research agents completed)

---

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

We analyzed 5 direct competitors to ForjeGames. Key strategic findings:

1. **ForjeGames has the most sophisticated AI architecture** (9-agent orchestrator, multi-provider, 9 modes) — no competitor matches this
2. **Ropilot is BYOK** (bring your own API key) — users pay Ropilot $20-250/mo AND pay OpenAI/Anthropic separately. ForjeGames including AI inference is a massive UX/pricing advantage
3. **Lemonade uses Roblox OAuth** — zero-friction auth for the target audience. We should add this
4. **Our $30/mo pricing is 2-3x competitors** — Rebirth is $8.99-15.99/mo, Ropilot starts at $20/mo (+ API costs)
5. **Nobody has our orchestrator, canvas editor, mesh generation, or checkpoint system** — these are genuine differentiators
6. **All competitors lack** multi-agent coordination, image generation depth (13 styles), 3D mesh, Robux payments

---
1. ROPILOT (ropilot.ai) — MOST TECHNICALLY ADVANCED COMPETITOR

Company
- **Entity**: ROSUITE, LLC, 131 Continental Dr Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713
- **Creator**: "ClassifiedCoach" (Twitter/GitHub) — started as open-source plugin Sept 2024, commercialized Jan 2026
- **Launched**: January 24, 2026 (~3 months ago)
- **Team**: Solo dev / very small team (vanilla JS frontend, unsigned installers)

Architecture (TWO components — critical difference from us)
1. **Ropilot Studio** = Desktop app (Windows/macOS/Linux) — the agent harness/orchestrator
2. **Roblox Studio Plugin** — lightweight, pairs with desktop app via MCP
3. **npm package**: `ropilot` v0.1.50 (MIT) — MCP CLI bridge for Claude/Cursor integration

AI Models — CRITICAL FINDING
**Ropilot is BYOK (Bring Your Own Key).** Users must have their own API keys from:
- Anthropic Claude
- OpenAI (GPT-5.4 shown in demos)
- Google Gemini (from open-source version)

**This means Ropilot charges for orchestration, NOT AI inference.** Users pay Ropilot $20-250/mo PLUS their own OpenAI/Anthropic bills ($20-50+/mo). Total cost: $40-300+/mo.

**ForjeGames advantage: All-inclusive pricing. One bill, one relationship.**

How Their AI Pipeline Works
1. Plugin traverses Roblox services (`GetDescendants()`), extracts all Script/LocalScript/ModuleScript source code
2. Combines static instruction schema + serialized codebase + user prompt
3. HTTP request to user's selected AI provider
4. Response parsed as structured JSON with `changes` array (action type, target script, line numbers, code)
5. Three action types: ModifyExisting (comments out original + inserts new), AddOnly, NewScript
6. Full undo history tracking
7. Automated playtest with input simulation + screenshot capture

Pricing
| Plan | Monthly | Annual | Actions/Month | Concurrent |
|------|---------|--------|--------------|------------|
| Free | $0 | $0 | 250 (~5 features) | 1 |
| Light | $20 | $16.70 | 6,750* | 1 |
| Pro | $50 | $41.70 | 22,500* | 2 |
| Max | $250 | $208.30 | 168,750* | 5 |
*Includes 50% launch bonus. AI inference NOT included — users pay separately.

Key Features We're Missing
- **Desktop app** (agent harness runs locally, not web)
- **MCP CLI npm package** (plug into Claude Code / Cursor)
- **User-selectable AI provider** in plugin settings
- **Input simulation** during playtest (keyboard/mouse automation via VirtualInputManager)
- **Invite code system** on login (controlled growth)

Strategic Assessment
Ropilot's BYOK model is their **biggest vulnerability**. Young Roblox developers don't want to manage API keys and separate billing. Our all-inclusive model is simpler. Their desktop app requirement adds friction vs our web-based approach. But their MCP integration with Claude Code/Cursor is innovative.

---
2. REBIRTH (userebirth.com) — STRONGEST TRACTION

Company
- **Location**: England (laws of England and Wales)
- **Traction Claims**: 50K+ users, 2M+ Robux earned from Rebirth-built games, 2,000+ prompts/day
- **Social**: @UseRebirthAI (Twitter), @userebirth (TikTok), Discord: discord.gg/RvJMuMnkHf
- **Contact**: support@userebirth.com
- **Founded**: ~Q1 2026 (blog content starts March 2026)

Tech Stack
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15 + Turbopack (same as us) |
| Auth | Supabase Auth (Google OAuth only) |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL + Realtime) |
| Backend/AI | Fly.io (vibegame1.fly.dev) |
| Payments | Stripe |
| Analytics | Umami (privacy-focused, self-hosted) |
| Affiliates | Rewardful (data-rewardful="14300a") |
| Animation | DotLottie WASM + custom WebGL shaders |
| Plugin | Creator Store (Asset ID 124244262358116) |

AI Model
- **NOT DISCLOSED** — deliberately hidden
- Claims "deep understanding of Roblox APIs and context awareness"
- Likely a multimodal model (GPT-4o or Claude) behind the Fly.io proxy
- Third-party providers "may use your data for training"

Pricing (cheapest competitor)
| Plan | Price | Credits/mo |
|------|-------|-----------|
| Free | $0 | 5 credits on signup |
| Hobby | $8.99/mo | 100 prompts |
| Pro | $15.99/mo | 300 prompts |
1 credit = 1 prompt regardless of task size. Earn more via Discord.

Best Practices They Recommend (reveals AI limitations)
- "Keep scripts modular — a 1000-line script is hard for AI to reason about" (context window limits)
- "Copy-paste exact error messages" (AI needs precise context)
- "Review security-critical code" (known quality gaps)
- "Chain small prompts" rather than one-shot complex builds (complex generation fails)

Named Competitors (from their blog)
- Lemonade — "generates code in text box, must copy-paste, no scene context"
- SuperbulletAI — "tokens run out fast on complex tasks"
- RMod — "requires more setup, smaller community"
- Roblox 4D Creation — "asset creation only, no scripts/UI"

Key Features We're Missing
- **Affiliate program** (Rewardful — professional tracking)
- **TikTok marketing** (active, reaching young audience)
- **SEO blog** (6 posts, coordinated March 2026 launch)
- **Fly.io separate compute** (isolates AI from web server)
- **$8.99 entry price** (we're 3.3x more expensive)
- **50K user traction** (if real)

---
3. LEMONADE (lemonade.gg) — CLOSEST STACK, BEST AUTH

Company
- **Location**: San Francisco
- **Founder**: @nicolasvizi (Twitter/X)
- **Traction Claims**: 100,000+ creators
- **Social**: @gg_lemonade, Discord: discord.com/invite/lemonade
- **Founded**: ~September 2025

Tech Stack (nearly identical to ours!)
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js (App Router) on Vercel |
| Auth | **Clerk** v6.33.1 (`pk_live_Y2xlcmsubGVtb25hZGUuZ2ck`) |
| Database | Supabase (server-side only — smart security) |
| Payments | Clerk Checkout + Stripe |
| Analytics | **Mixpanel** + **rrweb** session recording + Vercel Analytics/SpeedInsights |
| Plugin | Creator Store "Lemonade AI" (Asset ID 85716018250741) |

Auth — CRITICAL FINDING
**Lemonade uses "Sign in with Roblox"** as their PRIMARY auth. The sign-up page says: "Signing in will take you to the official Roblox website. We only see your display name and avatar."

**This is genius for the Roblox demographic. Every user already has a Roblox account. Zero friction.** We should add Roblox OAuth.

AI Model
- Uses "top-tier private models" — refuses to name them
- FAQ: claims to outperform "open-source models" used by Roblox Assistant
- Suspected **Gemini** (DevForum user mentioned "Gemini 3" in Lemonade context)
- Context augmentation: "We improve generation by selectively passing our own context data"
- Pipeline: Prompt received → Lemonade context data injected → Model generation

Pricing
| Plan | Price | Allocation |
|------|-------|-----------|
| Free | $0 | 1 credit/day (was 4 prompts) |
| Paid | $20/mo | 100 prompts |
**$0.20/prompt is EXPENSIVE** — SuperBulletAI offers 55x more at same price.

Key Features
- **Roblox OAuth** (zero-friction for target audience)
- **Agent Playtest** (autonomous test-and-fix loop)
- **Version Control** (rollback to any past prompt iteration)
- **Cross-device** (mobile-friendly web UI)
- **Console log mirroring** (view Studio output in browser)
- **Mixpanel + rrweb** (advanced user analytics + session replay)

What They CANNOT Do (per competitor comparison)
- No code diff/comparison view
- No 3D model generation
- No animation generation
- No VFX generation
- No UI icon generation
- Cannot make "production-ready" systems (described as "boilerplate only")
- 2-3 minute processing for complex prompts (SLOW)

---
4. FORGEGUI (forgegui.com) — UI/DESIGN NICHE

Company
- **Entity**: ForgeGUI, Inc. (formally incorporated)
- **Twitter/X**: @forgeguiai
- **Discord**: discord.gg/R6k7xSKG3V
- **Built with**: bolt.new (StackBlitz) — Project ID: 4bc6871d-7991-41e6-9116-6bceb500ad5a

Tech Stack
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Vite + React SPA |
| Auth | Supabase Auth (includes Web3/Solana wallet support!) |
| Database | Supabase (`azyjlkhpdgafuobyxoax.supabase.co`) v2.71.1 |
| Built with | bolt.new (AI code generation platform) |
| Dev server | localhost:9999 |

Supabase Tables/RPC Found in JS
- `client_errors` — error logging
- `get_public_user_count` — social proof counter
- `get_assets_generated_today` — daily volume tracking

Credit System
- Thumbnails/Icons: 4 credits each
- GUI assets: 3-5 credits (mostly 5)
- Edit/refine mode available
- Consistency system for style across assets

Key Features
- **Public remix gallery** (fork others' designs)
- **Prompt guide page** (teaching users)
- **Thumbnails, Icons, GUI assets, Clothing** generation
- **Style consistency** across generated assets
- **YouTube tutorials** embedded
- **Web3/Solana auth planned** (in Supabase config)

Assessment
ForgeGUI is NOT a real builder — web-only, no Studio plugin, no code execution. Built quickly with bolt.new. Weakest technical competitor. But their remix gallery and prompt guide are features worth copying.

---
5. BLOXTOOLKIT (bloxtoolkit.com) — BROADEST BUT FAKEST

Company
- Domain registered: January 14, 2026 (3 months old)
- **ScamAdviser trust score: 0/100** (flagged as risky)
- **FAKE TESTIMONIALS** — avatar images scraped from DevForum users, names fabricated ("Lena Sparks", "Skyler Hart", "Mara Quinn")
- No DevForum presence, no GitHub, no Twitter, no npm packages

Tech Stack
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js + Turbopack (marketing), separate app at app.bloxtoolkit.com |
| Auth | Custom email/password + Google OAuth (no third-party service) |
| Analytics | PostHog (full: session replay, surveys, dead click, web vitals) + GA4 |
| CDN | CloudFront |
| Fonts | Space Grotesk + Geist Mono |

Pricing (most expensive)
| Plan | Price | Credits | Images | Batches |
|------|-------|---------|--------|---------|
| Starter | $19/mo | 500 | 250 | 3/batch |
| Growth | $39/mo | 1,200 | 600 | 5/batch |
| Pro | $79/mo | 2,800 | 1,400 | 7/batch |

AI
- Idea Generator: explicitly "GPT-based" (OpenAI confirmed)
- Code Assistant: "Luau answers only, Roblox services enforced"
- Image Gen: Unknown model, 12 style presets, HD upscaling, reference image upload

Assessment
Not a serious competitor. Web-only, no Studio integration, fake testimonials, 3-month-old domain with 0/100 trust. But validates market willingness to pay $19-79/mo for Roblox AI tools.

---
